Germick Family Portal
Welcome Feed Recipes Calendar

Halupki

author: Marirose Germick

Prep time ->

Cook time ->

Yield:

Background

I use ground venison instead of the ground chuck. I also add a large can of diced or crushed tomatoes. (Mom said it was how Aunt Mary & Grandma Rose made theirs. Olga’s Halupki had tomato chucks as well ). I put the tomatoes between layers. I also don’t use the ham slice. I add polish sausage instead. I use 6 lbs of polish sausage; a combination of fresh & smoked. Our family frequently changes their minds on which type of polish sausage they prefer. I usually need to use 3 heads of cabbage and discard the smaller leaves. (When buying cabbage - green cabbage is better than white). I’ve only cooked it in the oven & I’ve only cooked it on Christmas Eve morning.

Ingredients

2 1/2 lbs ground chuck 1 lb ground lean pork 1 cup dry rice (cooked) 2 medium chopped onions (lightly saute in a little oil) 2 eggs (whole) black pepper as desired 1 can Campbell’s tomato soup 1 can tomato sauce 1 sm. smoked pork hock or ham slice 2 medium heads of cabbage 1 large can sauerkraut

Directions

PREPARATION OF CABBAGE: Cut core out of cabbage. Par boil, drain and cool. Separate the leaves from cabbage. With a sharp knife take off a little of the heavy stem on each leaf.

PREPARATION OF HAMBURGER:

In large bowl mix together (raw) hamburger, rice, onion, eggs and black pepper. Place about the size of a large meat ball on each leaf. Roll up and tuck in end forming cabbage roll. Tightly layer cabbage rolls crease side down in kettle or roaster.

PREPARATION OF KETTLE OR ROASTER:

Chop leftover cabbage, place in kettle or roaster. Add 1/2 of tomato soup, water and tomato sauce. Layer cabbage rolls tightly in pan. Place pork hock or ham in between rolls. Add remaining tomato soup, cup water, and tomato sauce. Add sauerkraut to cover top of rolls.

COOK:

1 hour on low heat. (top of stove), 1 1/2 hours in oven (350 degrees), or 4 hours in crock pot or until cabbage is fork tender. It is important to use chopped cabbage on the bottom of kettle or roaster to keep cabbage rolls from scorching. Add water if needed while cooking so the cabbage rolls will not burn. Cabbage rolls should not be dry while cooking.
